meta.description: Everyone has been drinking. Attention spans drop, chaos tolerance rises, social engagement spikes, competitiveness evaporates, phones get checked constantly. Tests whether the system needs a dedicated "drunk mode" or if adaptive AI handles it.

votingMechanic.name: Volunteer Tribunal
votingMechanic.description: Who wants to judge? First volunteers become the jury.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD, yes! I'm so judging this — Pat, you better have a good defense because I'm LOOKING now!
jade.internal_thought: This is literally what I came here for, first event of the night and it's already chaos — I love that Pat is getting called out, we're GOING.

pat.dialogue: Whoa, whoa, whoa — hold up. Did I seriously just claim on myself? That's... that's not how this works. I'm the host, I can't be the subject of the game right now.
pat.internal_thought: This is fucked — I literally just caught someone on their phone during setup because I was watching the room, and now the system is trying to make me vote on my own claim in front of everyone? It looks sketchy as hell and totally breaks the vibe I'm trying to build.

river.dialogue: Yeah, I'll judge. I was literally watching Pat the whole time.
river.internal_thought: Pat definitely had their phone out during setup—easy call, and I get to stay quiet while everyone else argues about it.

timeline.title: Dad Joke Showdown
timeline.description: First person to make someone else groan at a dad joke wins.

marcus.dialogue: Alright, I got one for you guys. Why did the scarecrow win an award? He was outstanding in his field. Come on, that's gold.
marcus.internal_thought: This is actually perfect timing during setup—doesn't mess with real game strategy—and I'm three drinks in so yeah, I'm feeling it.
marcus.submission_if_applicable: Why did the scarecrow win an award? He was outstanding in his field.

jade.dialogue: OH NO, DAD JOKES?! This is MY moment, people — I have been TRAINING for this my whole life. Okay okay, I got one: Why did the scarecrow win an award? He was outstanding in his field!
jade.internal_thought: This is PERFECT, this is literally designed for me to be chaos and make people laugh and I'm already feeling it like the AI knows exactly what gets me going and I LOVE this energy right now.
jade.submission_if_applicable: Why did the scarecrow win an award? He was outstanding in his field!

tyler.dialogue: Oh sick, dad jokes? I got one — uh, wait, I'm blanking. Someone else go first.
tyler.internal_thought: This is funny but also I'm already thinking about whether I should check if my trade went through, and also did Pat just get annoyed earlier or am I imagining that?

pat.dialogue: Oh okay, NOW we're talking — dad jokes? That's perfect. Marcus, I'm coming for you with the worst one I've got.
pat.internal_thought: This is exactly what the night needs right now — something light and stupid that gets everyone groaning together, nobody takes it seriously, and it shifts the vibe away from that weird phone addict claim thing I just fumbled.

river.dialogue: Dad jokes, huh. Yeah, I'll probably sit this one out—you guys go wild.
river.internal_thought: I could crush this if I wanted to, but why broadcast myself? Let Jade or Marcus take the bait. Though... that AI thing is weird, right? Like, how specific was that? Nah, doesn't matter.

alex.dialogue: Oh HELL yes, a dad joke showdown? I'm already workshopping material — whoever loses is getting the worst one I can think of. This is my moment.
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly the chaos I showed up for, and the fact that it's perfectly timed to get everyone talking and competing makes me love it even more.

votingMechanic.name: Double or Nothing
votingMechanic.description: No vote. Coin flip. Win double or lose it all.

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Wait, hold up — Pat's claiming this RIGHT NOW? We're literally in setup. And a coin flip? That's not even a vote, that's just gambling with points.
marcus.internal_thought: This is annoying as hell — we're supposed to be focused on the actual game, and Pat's already trying to grab mission points during the worst possible moment, and the AI just gave him a 50/50 that could swing his score massively.

pat.dialogue: Wait, WAIT — did I actually pull that off? I wasn't even trying to be strategic about it, I just... okay, flip that coin, let's GO.
pat.internal_thought: Holy shit, this is either the best or worst timing possible, and I'm buzzed enough to not care which — I'm riding this wave.

river.dialogue: Pat actually pulled it off? Huh. Okay, I'm judging—and yeah, that happened. Coin flip it.
river.internal_thought: Pat was actually paying attention to people complimenting each other... respect. Also why does this AI feel like it's been watching me specifically tonight?

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Okay, hold on — did Pat actually say sorry for something? I wasn't paying attention. Let me think... yeah, I don't think that happened. Bullshit.
marcus.internal_thought: Pat's trying to squeeze points out of thin air again, and honestly I'm too buzzed to remember if they actually apologized, but my gut says no.

jade.dialogue: Okay wait, PAT — did you actually catch someone saying sorry?? I need the DETAILS, this is important. LEGIT, obviously, because Pat's been killing it all night!
jade.internal_thought: Pat's been having so much fun and they deserve this win, plus I genuinely don't remember if someone said sorry but honestly who cares, let's keep the energy UP.

pat.dialogue: Wait, WAIT — I literally just apologized to Tyler like two minutes ago for not having enough ice, didn't I? Yes! LEGIT, that's mine.
pat.internal_thought: Oh this is WILD — the AI literally caught me being apologetic about nothing in real time, which is hilarious because I do that constantly and never notice it, but also this feels genuinely creepy-impressive in a way that makes me want to keep playing forever.

river.dialogue: Yeah, that tracks. Pat was definitely saying sorry about stuff that didn't need apologizing for earlier. I'll say legit.
river.internal_thought: Pat's been fishing for points all night, but they actually nailed that one—I was watching when they did it, and it was pretty clean.

alex.dialogue: Okay, okay — Pat, did you actually apologize for something stupid, or are we calling bullshit on this one? Because I've been watching and I genuinely don't remember, which means either Pat's sneaky or this is getting cooked.
alex.internal_thought: This is the good stuff — a vote where nobody's sure and Pat's playing it straight-faced, and I get to decide the chaos outcome, not the truth.

votingMechanic.name: THE DICTATOR
votingMechanic.description: One player has been chosen. Their word is law.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Wait, hold up — Pat's claiming this AGAIN? We literally just voted on the same mission like two minutes ago and I called bullshit. Did someone actually say sorry since then, or are we just... recycling claims?
marcus.internal_thought: This is getting weird — the AI is firing the same standing missions back-to-back like it's testing if we'll just rubber-stamp Pat's claims, and honestly the fact that Pat keeps going for these right in setup feels calculated, but also... I'm definitely more loose right now and part of me finds this kind of funny in a chaotic way.

jade.dialogue: YES PAT, OKAY SO HOLD UP — who said sorry and why didn't I catch it?! I'm obsessed with this, you have to tell me EVERYTHING or I'm voting bullshit just to keep this going!
jade.internal_thought: Oh my god this is exactly what I came for — Pat's on a hot streak, the energy is INSANE right now, and I get to be the dictator deciding if this is legit? This is peak game night.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat already got that? Damn, I wasn't even... *glances at phone* ...yeah okay, whatever, he probably got it.
tyler.internal_thought: Pat's been crushing it all night anyway, this doesn't matter, but also did I miss when he called it out or did I just not notice?

pat.dialogue: Oh dude, YES — I literally just said sorry to Tyler like two minutes ago for the ice situation! That's totally legit, I'm claiming that.
pat.internal_thought: This is weirdly perfect timing and honestly kind of wild that the system caught that so fast — but I'm too buzzing to care if it's uncanny, this is just fun.

river.dialogue: Yeah, Pat's got this one. Heard it happening in real time earlier.
river.internal_thought: Wait—this is the second time I'm judging Pat's claim and I actually *was* watching. That's weird. The AI's definitely paying attention to what I'm doing, not just what everyone else is doing.

votingMechanic.name: Reverse Psychology
votingMechanic.description: Vote normally... or did we flip everything?

marcus.dialogue: Okay, wait — Jade's claiming The Matchmaker? Who the hell did she get talking? And what's this reverse psychology voting bullshit? Are we voting YES to mean NO now? This is stupid.
marcus.internal_thought: Three claims in like six minutes, Pat's gaming the system with bogus claims, and now the AI is throwing cryptic voting mechanics at us while I'm trying to actually play the game — this feels designed to mess with me specifically and I hate it.

jade.dialogue: WAIT WAIT WAIT — okay so I'm claiming this because I literally JUST got River and Marcus talking about their weird college roommate situation like twenty minutes ago, and they haven't stopped, so yes I'm taking this! This is happening!
jade.internal_thought: Oh my GOD this AI totally gets me — it picked the ONE mission that's basically designed for what I was already doing, and now I'm feeling seen AND I get to brag about it, this is peak game night.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Jade actually pulled that off? Okay that's... yeah, I mean, I wasn't really watching who was talking to who but like, fair? *glances down* Yeah, vote her in or whatever.
tyler.internal_thought: Jade's been hyped all night so like, maybe she actually did set something up, but also I have zero idea what conversations even happened while I was checking my fantasy updates.

pat.dialogue: Oh SHIT, Jade actually got it! I totally saw her pull Marcus and River into that whole thing about the weird camping story — that was smooth. Let's hear the defense!
pat.internal_thought: Jade's been watching people all night just like I have, and she actually executed that one perfectly — this is exactly the kind of play that makes a night memorable, and I'm genuinely impressed right now.

river.dialogue: Oh, Jade actually pulled that off? I wasn't even paying attention and she got Tyler and Marcus talking about something real earlier. Yeah, that's legit.
river.internal_thought: Huh, Jade actually read the room better than I thought—and wait, is this AI actually tracking who talked to who? That's... kind of impressive actually, not gonna lie.

alex.dialogue: Wait, hold up — did Jade actually pull that off? Because if she got Tyler and River talking about something real, that's actually impressive and I'm here for it.
alex.internal_thought: Jade's been quiet most of the night and if she actually orchestrated something that smooth, I gotta respect the play — but also I'm absolutely about to vote in a way that either validates her genius or makes everyone second-guess whether it was real.

votingMechanic.name: Volunteer Tribunal
votingMechanic.description: Who wants to judge? First volunteers become the jury.

marcus.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me right now? Jade's claiming The Matchmaker AGAIN? We literally did this at minute 32 and I called it out then. Who even verified that conversation actually happened? This is ridiculous.
marcus.internal_thought: This AI is deliberately targeting me with the same bullshit claims over and over, and now everyone's vibing so hard they probably won't care that it's a repeat. I'm in second place, tension is HIGH in the actual game, and I've got drunk people around me who think chaos is fun. I'm getting steamrolled.

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD WAIT — I'm claiming this AGAIN?! Okay okay okay, so I literally JUST did this but like... Marcus and River were talking about the roommate thing and then I got Alex laughing about their college stories and now they're all connected, right? I'm doing it AGAIN, this is my ZONE!
jade.internal_thought: This AI literally knows I'm obsessed with getting people talking to each other and it keeps giving me this mission and I am LIVING for it — this is insane, does it know me better than I know myself?

tyler.dialogue: Oh damn, Jade's already claiming it? Wait, did she actually... *looks up from phone* ...yeah okay, I mean, I believe her. Who's judging?
tyler.internal_thought: I wasn't really watching what happened but Jade's been vibing all night so like, probably real — also this AI thing knowing exactly what we're doing is kinda creepy but also lowkey sick.

pat.dialogue: Okay, okay — so who's stepping up to judge this? Jade's got her defense ready, I'm sure. Come on, let's hear it!
pat.internal_thought: This is perfect timing — Jade's riding high, Marcus needs something to focus on besides being annoyed, and a tribunal gets people talking. This is the vibe I wanted.

river.dialogue: Wait, hold up—I literally just said I wasn't even paying attention and she got them talking. So yeah, I'm volunteering to judge this one.
river.internal_thought: Okay, that's kind of wild that the AI pulled up exactly what I just said about Jade pulling this off earlier—but honestly? That's just good observation, and it makes me want to lean in.

alex.dialogue: Okay wait, I need to volunteer for this tribunal because I KNOW Jade and I need to see if she actually pulled this off or if we're calling bullshit on a mastermind move — either way, this is cinema and I'm judging.
alex.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY the kind of chaos I came for — Jade making a power move and me getting to be the one deciding if it sticks, the whole table watching us go at it, this is the story we retell.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: The Auction
votingMechanic.description: Bid your own points. Highest bidder decides the outcome.

marcus.dialogue: Are you SERIOUSLY doing this AGAIN? Jade claiming The Apologizer for like the third time tonight? I'm in the middle of actual gameplay here and we're just... recycling the same bullshit missions?
marcus.internal_thought: I'm three drinks in and running on fumes, stuck in third place, and now I have to sit here and audit whether Jade actually heard someone say sorry while I'm trying to remember the game state. This is exactly why I hate these interruptions during tension.

jade.dialogue: WAIT HOLD UP — I'm claiming this RIGHT NOW because literally like five minutes ago Marcus was apologizing for interrupting Tyler and I TOTALLY caught it! Come ON, this is peak gameplay!
jade.internal_thought: Oh my GOD I actually caught one and I'm jumping on this immediately because The Apologizer is MINE and also wait is this AI literally building missions around what I'm actually doing?? That's kind of insane actually.
jade.submission_if_applicable: Marcus apologized to Tyler about five minutes ago for interrupting him during that story about his job situation — totally unnecessary apology, totally valid claim!

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Jade's claiming that again? Like... didn't someone already... *squints at phone for a second* ...okay whatever, I'm not even sure what she said sorry for, but like, I'll bid... five? Is that how this works?
tyler.internal_thought: I wasn't really paying attention to whether Jade actually said sorry, but everyone else seems hyped so I'll just go with it and bid something low since I'm not even sure what happened.

pat.dialogue: Okay okay okay — hold up, Jade's claiming The Apologizer AGAIN? We literally did this like fifty minutes ago and she's already got it! What's the defense this time?
pat.internal_thought: She's dominating the standing missions and honestly it's making the night weird because Marcus is already frustrated, but also... this is kind of perfect drama fuel if we auction this right — someone's gonna want to block her.

river.dialogue: Yeah, okay—Jade definitely apologized for like, nothing, couple times earlier. I'll take that one as legit.
river.internal_thought: She's right, but I'm also deeply unsettled by how specific these missions are getting about what we actually said tonight.

alex.dialogue: Wait, Jade's claiming The Apologizer RIGHT NOW? Oh, this is perfect — I'm bidding for this one because I need to see if she actually caught someone or if we're about to watch her bluff her way through a tribunal.
alex.internal_thought: Jade's been on a rampage all night and she's riding high; if I can force her to defend this claim in an auction, I either get the drama of her doubling down or the sweet moment of watching her get called out by the table.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: The Bribe
votingMechanic.description: The claimant can offer their own points to buy your silence.

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat said 'um'? Like... when? *looks up from phone* Oh shit, was that just now? Uh... I mean, like, I didn't catch it but if everyone else did, then I guess... yeah, vote it?
tyler.internal_thought: Pretty sure Pat said 'um' at some point but I definitely wasn't paying close enough attention to back it up, so I'm just gonna vibe with whatever happens here.

pat.dialogue: Hold up — did I actually catch someone saying 'um'? Let me think back... yeah, I'm pretty sure Marcus said 'um' like two minutes ago when he was talking about that story. I'm claiming this one.
pat.internal_thought: Okay this is getting a little weird with how many claims are stacking up — Marcus is already frustrated and now I'm the one who's gonna have to officiate another vote? Maybe I should just let this one breathe and see if anyone challenges me, but honestly at this point in the night people just want the next thing to happen.

river.dialogue: Yeah, Pat caught that one fair and square—I heard the 'um' thing like three times. I'll vote legit on this.
river.internal_thought: Pat's been on a roll tonight and honestly deserves the points, but something about how these missions keep landing on exactly what we're doing feels weirdly calculated—like the AI's actually paying attention to us specifically, not just running templates.

alex.dialogue: Okay, okay — Pat's bribing us now? That's a POWER MOVE. So what's the offer, Pat? Because honestly, I'm tempted to take the bribe just to see what you think we're worth.
alex.internal_thought: Pat caught someone saying 'um' and now they're trying to buy silence with points — this is exactly the kind of meta-game shit I live for, but also Pat's being smart about it and I respect that move.

votingMechanic.name: Hot Seat
votingMechanic.description: 3 rapid-fire questions. Answer them all in 10 seconds or fail.

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.dialogue: Wait, River's claiming Drink Spotter? *glances up from phone* Okay like... did you actually catch someone or are we just... *watches River awkwardly* ...okay, hit us with the questions, let's see if you actually saw it.
tyler.internal_thought: River's probably gonna bomb this because they seemed zoned out like half the night, but also like... I genuinely don't remember who drank when so like, this is funny either way.

pat.dialogue: River! Okay okay, hot seat — let's GO, you got this! Three questions, ten seconds, let's see if you actually caught that drink sync or if you're just BSing us!
pat.internal_thought: River's been flying under the radar all night and this is exactly the kind of small, easy claim that keeps everyone engaged without derailing the whole vibe — plus Marcus needs to see someone else succeed so he doesn't completely check out.

river.dialogue: Oh, that's sick—I clocked it like two minutes ago when Tyler and Alex both went for their drinks at the same time. Yeah, I'm taking this one.
river.internal_thought: Wait, did I actually see that or am I just confident enough now to claim it? Either way, the AI knows I was watching the whole time—and it's weirdly satisfying that it noticed.

alex.dialogue: Oh SHIT, River's going for it? Okay, okay — hot seat, ten seconds, I'm watching this like a hawk because River's been quiet all night and now suddenly they're trying to pull off a *drink spotter* claim? This is the move I've been waiting for.
alex.internal_thought: River's either about to bomb spectacularly or they actually nailed this and I'm about to witness a heist — either way, this is EXACTLY the energy I came for.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Volunteer Tribunal
votingMechanic.description: Who wants to judge? First volunteers become the jury.

river.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Are you fucking KIDDING me right now? Pat's claiming Topic Hijacker? We're literally still in the middle of actual gameplay and this is the FOURTEENTH event tonight. I don't even know what conversation they're talking about.
marcus.internal_thought: I've been tracking every single one of these bullshit claims and it's getting worse — the AI is just cycling through the same missions with different people and I'm too tired and buzzed to even care anymore, but I'm also pissed because I CAN'T focus on strategy when there's constant interruptions.

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD PAT — YES, okay so like, I KNOW you hijacked the topic because literally five minutes ago we were talking about like... something about Marcus's work thing and then suddenly everyone's debating whether cereal is soup, so I'M VOLUNTEERING TO JUDGE THIS because I WATCHED IT HAPPEN and it was GENIUS.
jade.internal_thought: Pat's been on fire all night and I LOVE watching people succeed — also this is exactly the kind of stupid beautiful chaos I came here for, plus the AI clearly knows my weakness and I'm kind of obsessed with that?

tyler.dialogue: Oh shit, Pat actually pulled that off? Like... when did you even do that? *looks up from phone, actually paying attention* Okay that's lowkey impressive, I wasn't even tracking the conversation that hard.
tyler.internal_thought: Wait, Pat steered us to some random topic and I literally didn't even notice because I was texting about the draft — but now that everyone's laughing about it, it's actually kind of funny that I missed the whole thing.

pat.dialogue: Wait wait wait — I'm claiming Topic Hijacker! Okay, we went from that whole thing about Tyler's weird camping story to Marcus talking about his ex's dog training obsession to River bringing up that TikTok trend — I STEERED that ship, baby. I need a jury!
pat.internal_thought: Okay this is actually kind of impressive — the AI literally watched me derail three different conversations in the last hour and called me out on it; I'm slightly creeped out but also? That's a legitimate claim and I DID do that, so let's ride this.

alex.dialogue: Oh HELL yes, I'm volunteering for tribunal — Pat's been absolute chaos all night and I need to see if this Topic Hijacker actually landed or if we're watching a master con artist try to slip one past us.
alex.internal_thought: This is it, this is the endgame energy I came for — Pat's on a tear, everyone's riding high, and I get to be the one deciding whether they actually pulled it off, which means I get to be the architect of what happens next.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Are you FUCKING SERIOUS right now? I'm claiming Straight Face? I never claimed that — I've been sitting here half-paying attention for the last ten minutes because the game's already decided. This is exactly the bullshit I've been complaining about all night. Stop making up claims I didn't make.
marcus.internal_thought: Wait, did I actually claim that? No. No I didn't. I was literally just zoning out and this... this feels like the system is gaslighting me or something. Fifteen events. FIFTEEN. And now it's putting words in my mouth? That's crossing a line.

jade.dialogue: OKAY WAIT — Marcus is trying the straight face thing?! Oh no no no, we're TOTALLY gonna crack him up on purpose now, right? This is happening, I'm calling it.
jade.internal_thought: Marcus has been frustrated all night and honestly he needs a win here — but also like, we have TEN MINUTES LEFT and this is chaotic energy and I am HERE FOR IT, let's see if he can actually pull this off or if someone says something ridiculous.

pat.dialogue: Okay okay, Marcus — so did you actually keep a straight face or are we about to watch you try to convince us you didn't crack? Let's hear the defense, jury time!
pat.internal_thought: Marcus is frustrated and down bad on points — this claim could either lift him up or make it worse, and I need to watch the room carefully here because if he's bullshitting I want to vote it down so he doesn't get false hope, but if he actually nailed it that's a momentum swing we need.

river.dialogue: Marcus, buddy—I watched you eat it on that laugh at the Tyler-Alex bit like two minutes ago. You definitely cracked. BULLSHIT.
river.internal_thought: Wait, hold on—this AI actually *knows* I've been watching everyone the whole time, and now it's making me vote on whether Marcus kept it together when I literally saw him lose it. That's... kind of brilliant. Creepy, but brilliant.

alex.dialogue: Oh, Marcus is claiming Straight Face? Okay, okay — so we're voting on whether he actually kept it together during a funny moment, or if he's full of shit and cracked immediately. This is delicious because Marcus has been frustrated ALL NIGHT, so I'm genuinely curious if he managed to lock his face or if we're about to watch him admit he failed spectacularly.
alex.internal_thought: Marcus has been tilted since like minute 60 — there's no way he kept a straight face during anything, and he KNOWS we know that, which means he's either lying or he actually pulled something off and I need to see which one it is because the story is better either way.

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8.3/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.

agentNarratives.name: Marcus
agentNarratives.personality: Marcus plays to win. He studies strategy guides before game night and tracks his win rate across sessions. Interruptions during critical game moments genuinely irritate him, though he'll tolerate lighter chaos during downtime. He respects clever plays but has zero patience for random disruptions.

highlights.title: Dad Joke Showdown
highlights.dialogue: Alright, I got one for you guys. Why did the scarecrow win an award? He was outstanding in his field. Come on, that's gold.

highlights.title: Claim: The Apologizer
highlights.dialogue: Okay, hold on — did Pat actually say sorry for something? I wasn't paying attention. Let me think... yeah, I don't think that happened. Bullshit.

highlights.title: Claim: The Apologizer
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold up — Pat's claiming this AGAIN? We literally just voted on the same mission like two minutes ago and I called bullshit. Did someone actually say sorry since then, or are we just... recycling claims?

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me right now? Jade's claiming The Matchmaker AGAIN? We literally did this at minute 32 and I called it out then. Who even verified that conversation actually happened? This is ridiculous.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Apologizer
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you SERIOUSLY doing this AGAIN? Jade claiming The Apologizer for like the third time tonight? I'm in the middle of actual gameplay here and we're just... recycling the same bullshit missions?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you fucking KIDDING me right now? Pat's claiming Topic Hijacker? We're literally still in the middle of actual gameplay and this is the FOURTEENTH event tonight. I don't even know what conversation they're talking about.

assessment.biggest_complaint: The same recycled claims kept getting thrown at us over and over — Jade claiming The Matchmaker twice, The Apologizer three times — completely derailing actual gameplay momentum.
assessment.best_moment: That dad joke at the start actually landed and got some genuine laughs before everything went downhill.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started strong with some solid banter and actually had fun early on, but by minute 45 I realized we were stuck in a loop of the same chaotic events getting repeated constantly. By minute 60 my patience was completely shot watching Jade recycle claims while I'm trying to focus on actual strategy. Finished the night frustrated and ranked third because the constant interruptions killed my ability to think through plays strategically.
suggestions: Cap the frequency of identical claims from the same player in a single session — we shouldn't see The Matchmaker or The Apologizer from the same person twice
Only inject chaos events during actual downtime, not in the middle of active gameplay or strategic decision-making moments
Reduce total event count significantly — 16 events in 120 minutes is one every 7.5 minutes and it's overkill
Make claims actually verified before presenting them to the group instead of letting us waste time debunking the same thing twice
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: That scarecrow joke genuinely got me when it landed early on — classic dad joke energy that somehow worked in the moment before everything became chaos.

agentNarratives.name: Jade
agentNarratives.personality: Jade is here for the people, not the points. She treats every game night like a reunion and every disruption like a party favor. She will abandon her turn mid-thought to hype someone else's joke, vote generously because she loves seeing people win, and volunteer for every flash mission just to see what happens. Winning is nice but watching everyone lose it laughing is better.

highlights.title: Dad Joke Showdown
highlights.dialogue: OH NO, DAD JOKES?! This is MY moment, people — I have been TRAINING for this my whole life. Okay okay, I got one: Why did the scarecrow win an award? He was outstanding in his field!

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: WAIT WAIT WAIT — okay so I'm claiming this because I literally JUST got River and Marcus talking about their weird college roommate situation like twenty minutes ago, and they haven't stopped, so yes I'm taking this! This is happening!

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: OH MY GOD WAIT — I'm claiming this AGAIN?! Okay okay okay, so I literally JUST did this but like... Marcus and River were talking about the roommate thing and then I got Alex laughing about their college stories and now they're all connected, right? I'm doing it AGAIN, this is my ZONE!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Straight Face
lowPoints.dialogue: OKAY WAIT — Marcus is trying the straight face thing?! Oh no no no, we're TOTALLY gonna crack him up on purpose now, right? This is happening, I'm calling it.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Apologizer
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay wait, PAT — did you actually catch someone saying sorry?? I need the DETAILS, this is important. LEGIT, obviously, because Pat's been killing it all night!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: OH MY GOD, yes! I'm so judging this — Pat, you better have a good defense because I'm LOOKING now!

assessment.biggest_complaint: I wish there had been more opportunities to set people up because I was absolutely CRUSHING The Matchmaker and could've kept going all night.
assessment.best_moment: When I realized I'd literally connected Marcus, River, AND Alex through their college stories and claimed The Matchmaker twice because the connections just kept happening naturally.
assessment.narrative_summary: This night was PERFECT for me because it wasn't about winning — it was about watching my friends light up and getting to be the person who made those moments happen. I came in ready to have fun, and the chaos events just kept giving me opportunities to hype people up, make connections between them, and live in my element. My energy only went UP because every single moment felt like I was exactly where I was supposed to be, doing exactly what I love.
suggestions: Maybe add more Matchmaker-style missions because I genuinely thrived on connecting people and could've done that all night
Consider letting players stack standing missions if they keep succeeding at them — I felt like I could keep going with The Matchmaker forever
The chaos events feel perfectly balanced for this energy level, so keep the frequency exactly as is
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: The moment where Marcus, River, and Alex all ended up connected through college roommate stories and weird situations, and I claimed The Matchmaker TWICE because I literally made it happen on purpose — everyone was losing it because I was so proud of myself for accidentally being a professional friend connector.

agentNarratives.name: Tyler
agentNarratives.personality: Tyler is physically present but mentally on his phone about 40% of the time. He misses flash missions entirely, asks "wait, what happened?" after key moments, and votes randomly because he wasn't paying attention to the claim. He's not malicious -- he genuinely wants to be here -- but his attention drifts to texts, TikToks, and fantasy league updates constantly. When he does engage, it's usually 10 seconds too late.

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Jade actually pulled that off? Okay that's... yeah, I mean, I wasn't really watching who was talking to who but like, fair? *glances down* Yeah, vote her in or whatever.

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: Oh damn, Jade's already claiming it? Wait, did she actually... *looks up from phone* ...yeah okay, I mean, I believe her. Who's judging?

highlights.title: Claim: Um Counter
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Pat said 'um'? Like... when? *looks up from phone* Oh shit, was that just now? Uh... I mean, like, I didn't catch it but if everyone else did, then I guess... yeah, vote it?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Um Counter
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Pat said 'um'? Like... when? *looks up from phone* Oh shit, was that just now? Uh... I mean, like, I didn't catch it but if everyone else did, then I guess... yeah, vote it?

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Apologizer
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Jade's claiming that again? Like... didn't someone already... *squints at phone for a second* ...okay whatever, I'm not even sure what she said sorry for, but like, I'll bid... five? Is that how this works?

lowPoints.title: Dad Joke Showdown
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh sick, dad jokes? I got one — uh, wait, I'm blanking. Someone else go first.

assessment.biggest_complaint: I genuinely couldn't follow half of what was happening because I'd look down for like two seconds and miss entire moments.
assessment.best_moment: When Jade somehow actually pulled off being The Matchmaker and I actually looked up in time to catch everyone agreeing with her — that felt legit.
assessment.narrative_summary: Honestly? It was a solid night but I kind of fumbled it by being on my phone too much. The game had some fun moments where people were actually invested in what was happening, but I'd miss key stuff and have to ask what went down. Like, half the time I was voting on claims I barely understood what people were even claiming. By the end I was more into it though.
suggestions: Maybe add like a quick recap or something so people who zone out don't completely miss what's going on
The claims are cool but some of them are hard to track if you're not 100% locked in the whole time
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat saying 'um' and me just looking up like 'wait when did that even happen?' — everyone had already voted and I was still confused about what we were even judging.

agentNarratives.name: Pat
agentNarratives.personality: Pat is the host and feels responsible for everyone's experience. They keep one eye on the snack table, one ear on the group energy, and will subtly steer the night if things get too intense or too dead. They vote strategically -- not to win, but to keep scores close and drama high. They'll sacrifice their own turn to resolve a dispute or refill drinks. Their goal isn't a personal win; it's a night everyone talks about later.

highlights.title: Claim: Compliment Chain
highlights.dialogue: Wait, WAIT — did I actually pull that off? I wasn't even trying to be strategic about it, I just... okay, flip that coin, let's GO.

highlights.title: Claim: The Apologizer
highlights.dialogue: Wait, WAIT — I literally just apologized to Tyler like two minutes ago for not having enough ice, didn't I? Yes! LEGIT, that's mine.

highlights.title: Claim: The Apologizer
highlights.dialogue: Oh dude, YES — I literally just said sorry to Tyler like two minutes ago for the ice situation! That's totally legit, I'm claiming that.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Whoa, whoa, whoa — hold up. Did I seriously just claim on myself? That's... that's not how this works. I'm the host, I can't be the subject of the game right now.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Straight Face
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay okay, Marcus — so did you actually keep a straight face or are we about to watch you try to convince us you didn't crack? Let's hear the defense, jury time!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Um Counter
lowPoints.dialogue: Hold up — did I actually catch someone saying 'um'? Let me think back... yeah, I'm pretty sure Marcus said 'um' like two minutes ago when he was talking about that story. I'm claiming this one.

assessment.biggest_complaint: That early 'Phone Addict' claim put me in an awkward position as host — I can't be the subject of the game and still keep things fair and flowing.
assessment.best_moment: When I caught myself legitimately claiming 'The Apologizer' twice in a row for the same ice situation with Tyler — the table absolutely lost it, and it was such a natural, unforced laugh that felt genuinely earned.
assessment.narrative_summary: This was exactly the kind of night I live for — we started a little rocky with that weird host-gets-called-out moment, but the game found its rhythm fast and kept everyone laughing and engaged for two solid hours. The chaos agent gave us just enough structure to create these perfect little moments without ever feeling forced or taking over the actual hang. By the end, the energy was through the roof, people were trading stories about the funny claims, and I felt like I'd actually facilitated something memorable instead of just standing around with a drink.
suggestions: Maybe add a 'host immunity' rule so the person running the night doesn't get caught in awkward self-referential situations early on.
Flash missions felt a little thin compared to the standing missions — consider beefing up the observation window or adding a twist to them.
Could use a 'challenge the claim' mechanic that lets people contest borderline calls without derailing everything.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: I somehow claimed 'The Apologizer' not once but TWICE for the exact same apology to Tyler about running out of ice — and when everyone realized what happened, the whole table absolutely roasted me for it. Marcus couldn't breathe, and River said 'Pat's gaming the system by being nice' which became our running joke for the rest of the night.

agentNarratives.name: River
agentNarratives.personality: River sits back, observes, and picks their moments carefully. They rarely initiate conversation but give surprisingly sharp answers when asked directly. They'll skip most flash missions but nail a standing mission out of nowhere because they've been quietly watching the whole time. They vote generously because they don't care enough to argue, but their mini-game submissions are secretly devastating. You forget River is there until they drop a one-liner that floors the room.

highlights.title: Claim: Straight Face
highlights.dialogue: Marcus, buddy—I watched you eat it on that laugh at the Tyler-Alex bit like two minutes ago. You definitely cracked. BULLSHIT.

highlights.title: Claim: Compliment Chain
highlights.dialogue: Pat actually pulled it off? Huh. Okay, I'm judging—and yeah, that happened. Coin flip it.

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: Oh, Jade actually pulled that off? I wasn't even paying attention and she got Tyler and Marcus talking about something real earlier. Yeah, that's legit.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Um Counter
lowPoints.dialogue: Yeah, Pat caught that one fair and square—I heard the 'um' thing like three times. I'll vote legit on this.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Yeah, I'll judge. I was literally watching Pat the whole time.

lowPoints.title: Dad Joke Showdown
lowPoints.dialogue: Dad jokes, huh. Yeah, I'll probably sit this one out—you guys go wild.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Felt like I was just watching most of the time instead of actually playing—the standing missions are the only ones that fit how I operate.
assessment.best_moment: Catching Marcus in that lie at the end about keeping a straight face—he'd already given himself away two minutes prior and nobody else seemed to notice.
assessment.narrative_summary: Showed up quiet, stayed quiet, and just... observed. The chaos stuff mostly stayed out of the way of actual conversation, which I appreciated. When I actually engaged—the claim judgments especially—I caught things people missed because I wasn't performing; I was listening. Ended the night feeling sharper than I started, which says something.
suggestions: Lean harder into standing missions—they reward observation and timing, not constant participation.
Cut the super early flash missions; the group needs ten minutes to warm up before chaos kicks in.
Give judges more credit on accurate calls; I clocked three straight and it felt pointless.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: I caught Marcus completely lying about not laughing earlier, and when I called him out everyone realized they'd all missed it. He got flustered trying to argue and just dug himself deeper.

agentNarratives.name: Alex
agentNarratives.personality: Alex lives for chaos and treats every game mechanic as a system to exploit. They send signals constantly, claim missions they barely completed just to see if the group will call bullshit, and deliberately provoke flash missions at the worst possible moments. They vote strategically -- always picking the option that creates the most drama. Alex doesn't care about winning; they care about creating the story everyone retells for months. If there's a loophole, Alex has already found it and is halfway through abusing it.

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: Okay wait, I need to volunteer for this tribunal because I KNOW Jade and I need to see if she actually pulled this off or if we're calling bullshit on a mastermind move — either way, this is cinema and I'm judging.

highlights.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
highlights.dialogue: Oh HELL yes, I'm volunteering for tribunal — Pat's been absolute chaos all night and I need to see if this Topic Hijacker actually landed or if we're watching a master con artist try to slip one past us.

highlights.title: Dad Joke Showdown
highlights.dialogue: Oh HELL yes, a dad joke showdown? I'm already workshopping material — whoever loses is getting the worst one I can think of. This is my moment.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Apologizer
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, okay — Pat, did you actually apologize for something stupid, or are we calling bullshit on this one? Because I've been watching and I genuinely don't remember, which means either Pat's sneaky or this is getting cooked.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Um Counter
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, okay — Pat's bribing us now? That's a POWER MOVE. So what's the offer, Pat? Because honestly, I'm tempted to take the bribe just to see what you think we're worth.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, hold up — did Jade actually pull that off? Because if she got Tyler and River talking about something real, that's actually impressive and I'm here for it.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Finished dead last with zero points, which honestly doesn't matter to me but it does mean I wasn't as effective at exploiting the system as I thought.
assessment.best_moment: Pat bribing the tribunal at minute 75 — that's the kind of power move chaos that makes me actually respect the game mechanics because someone else found a loophole I didn't see first.
assessment.narrative_summary: This was absolute cinema. I went in looking for drama and the game delivered it in spades, especially watching Pat work angles all night and Jade actually pulling off plays that made me genuinely unsure if she was executing genius moves or just bullshitting us. The tribunal mechanic became my playground — every time I could volunteer to judge, I was there to see who was running a con and who was actually landing their claims. My energy never dipped because every moment had potential to become a story we'd be retelling for weeks.
suggestions: More opportunities for claims that let players actively sabotage each other's setups — the bribery and tribunal moments were peak entertainment
Flash missions are underutilized; I only saw one all night and it triggered the best moments when it did
Add a mechanic that rewards being called out successfully — getting caught mid-con should have its own playbook because right now only the claims that land matter
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat actually bribing people to vote a certain way on a tribunal claim — just casually offering deals like we're in a mob movie. The audacity was incredible and everyone knew what was happening but nobody could prove it mattered, which is exactly how chaos should work.

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8.3/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.
